target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
Closes #61661
Related #61661

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Control UI reloads could reopen a stale historical routed session from older browser-local routed keys instead of returning users to the real `main` session. The fix also needs to preserve valid scoped `sessionsByGateway` routed selections for the active gateway so supported routed restores keep working.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change confines the behavior fix to Control UI restore sanitization. Historical top-level routed keys are normalized back to `main`, while valid per-gateway routed selections are preserved explicitly so compatible routed restores remain sticky. Live gateway routing, backend session semantics, and broader routed-session persistence policy changes remain out of scope.

## User Impact

Users who reload the Control UI with legacy routed session storage no longer land in an obsolete routed conversation by mistake. Valid scoped routed selections for the current gateway still survive reload, so real routed restores keep their intended behavior instead of being flattened.

## Evidence

- Existing real-browser proof for this branch is preserved below under `## Real behavior proof`.
- The current head `2d718489a7da952b7188d3843e13d0e5a0ee5f2e` is a readability-only follow-up in the same restore helper and keeps the proved restore behavior.
- Current-head validation remains:
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental false --pretty false`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s lint`
  - `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
  - `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI could reload into a stale historical routed session from older persisted top-level settings instead of reopening on the real `main` session, while valid scoped per-gateway routed selections still needed to survive reload.
- Real environment tested: isolated temporary OpenClaw gateway plus a real Control UI browser session on `pi-pr-lab` / piclaw for branch proof commit `afa2413e82`; current head `2d718489a7da952b7188d3843e13d0e5a0ee5f2e` is a readability-only follow-up in the same restore helper and preserves the proved restore behavior.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Started an isolated temporary OpenClaw gateway for the PR branch.
  2. Seeded Control UI storage with the historical routed direct/thread key `agent:main:telegram:direct:12345:thread:12345:99`.
  3. Loaded the real Control UI in a browser and connected it to the temporary gateway.
  4. Repeated the reload flow with a scoped `sessionsByGateway` routed key for the same gateway.
  5. Revalidated current head `2d718489a7` with `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`, `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental false --pretty false`, `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s lint`, `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`, and `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main`.
- Evidence after fix: copied live browser proof already recorded on this PR shows `OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (f49955f)` running in an isolated temp gateway, successful RPCs `connect`, `sessions.subscribe`, `agents.list`, and `chat.history`, the UI restoring to `/chat?session=main`, the rendered page showing the main Chat session, and the historical routed key absent from the rendered DOM after restore. The same branch also carries browser-level Control UI E2E coverage in `ui/src/ui/e2e/session-restore.e2e.test.ts` for both the legacy top-level reset path and the scoped per-gateway preservation path.
- Observed result after fix: legacy top-level routed session values reopen on `main` instead of reviving a stale routed session, scoped per-gateway routed selections still restore for the active gateway, and the current exact head is back to maintainer-review state after the proof-capable review.
- What was not tested: no separate fresh manual browser run was repeated after the readability-only `2d718489a7` follow-up because that change only replaced sparse destructuring with explicit indexed locals inside `isHistoricalRoutedSessionKey`; no live third-party production gateway/account was used.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this worker container cannot launch local Chromium because `libglib-2.0.so.0` is missing, so the durable real-browser proof comes from the isolated `pi-pr-lab` run already attached to the PR history and the current head is validated locally with focused UI checks plus a clean merge-tree.
- Before evidence: before this fix, current main and the latest release line could restore the stored top-level routed `sessionKey` unchanged, which reopened a stale routed session after reload instead of returning the UI to `main`.

## Tests and validation

- `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s lint`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main`
- Added browser-level Control UI reload coverage in `ui/src/ui/e2e/session-restore.e2e.test.ts` for both the legacy top-level routed-key reset and scoped per-gateway routed-key preservation paths.
- Expanded `ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts` coverage for valid and invalid historical routed restore shapes.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change: Yes.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior change: Yes.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change: No.
- Highest-risk area: compatibility-sensitive Control UI restore behavior for existing browser-local settings that still contain legacy top-level routed session keys.
- Risk mitigation: the classifier change is confined to restore-time sanitization, valid scoped per-gateway routed selections are preserved explicitly, and both storage-level and browser-level regression coverage exercise the affected reload paths.

## Current review state

Maintainer review on current head `2d718489a7da952b7188d3843e13d0e5a0ee5f2e`; this body refresh preserves the existing routed-session proof and current-head validation without changing branch code.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: Open, Download, and Copy.
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably.
- Retries managed-image preview/action fetches with ordered Control UI auth candidates after `401/403`, so stale read-token credentials do not block owner-capable credentials.
- Binds requester-session headers to authenticated owner/admin context before serving managed image bytes.
- Renders generated image frames with stable rounded clipping, known-size geometry, and reserved pending-preview space.
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract.
- Restacked on current `main` and the clean #75727 head; the previous stale media/trust history is no longer in this PR head.
- Removed the public `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` config surface; Control UI thumbnails use the internal default.

## What Problem This Solves

Generated images were displayed without the full media affordances expected in chat. The implementation needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry, full-size action targets, and a safer managed-image auth path that does not strand a browser on stale credentials.

## Evidence

- Current head tested: `3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066`.
- Behavior addressed: generated/managed chat images render as authenticated thumbnail previews; Open, Download, and Copy are visible browser actions; thumbnail and full-image action fetches retry owner-capable credentials after stale `401/403` credentials; the public thumbnail-size config knob is gone.
- Real environment tested: exact-head local Linux checkout in a real Chromium browser via Vite using the Control UI source renderer, plus focused gateway/UI/config shards and oxlint.

Commands run after the current fix/proof:

```bash
git diff --check
rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node .artifacts/pr77017-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live Chromium image-action evidence:

```json
{
  "head": "3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066",
  "readyMarkerVisible": true,
  "imageRenderedFromBlob": true,
  "actionLabels": ["Open image", "Download image", "Copy image"],
  "thumbnailAuthHeaders": ["Bearer session-token", "Bearer owner-token"],
  "fullAuthHeaders": [
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token"
  ],
  "requesterSessionHeaders": ["agent:main:main"],
  "openedUrl": "blob:http://127.0.0.1:19890/...",
  "downloadedName": "Generated image proof.png",
  "copiedTypes": ["image/png"]
}
```

Additional local evidence:

```text
$ rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
# no matches

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 127.90s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 304.41s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` verifies managed-image thumbnail and full-image action fetches try `Bearer session-token`, receive `403`, then retry and succeed with `Bearer owner-token` while preserving `x-openclaw-requester-session-key`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` verifies ordered unique auth candidates.
- `src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` verifies bounded thumbnail generation without exposing a public config key.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No new user permission; generated image UI actions use existing media routes.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. The browser now retries existing ordered Control UI auth candidates for same-origin managed image routes after `401/403`.
- Data access scope changed? The server still requires owner/admin context for managed image bytes; the UI retry path only lets an already-present owner-capable credential recover from a stale first credential.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No. The proposed public thumbnail-size config was removed before merge.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: docs(memory): add memory system deep dive guide
target.body:
## Summary

Add a comprehensive memory system deep dive guide, addressing #43032.

### Problem

`docs/concepts/memory.md` covers basics (~600 words) but lacks depth on memory best practices, search optimization, lifecycle management, and advanced patterns.

### New document: `docs/concepts/memory-deep-dive.md` (401 lines)

Covers all 6 sections requested in the issue:

1. **Memory Architecture** — Two-layer model (`MEMORY.md` + `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`), indexing scope, what is searchable vs not
2. **Writing Effective Memory** — What belongs in MEMORY.md vs daily logs, promotion workflow, formatting tips for better search indexing
3. **Search Optimization** — Hybrid retrieval pipeline (keyword + vector + MMR), query strategies (semantic vs exact), `memory_search` → `memory_get` workflow, tuning parameters (`maxResults`, `minScore`)
4. **Memory Maintenance** — Heartbeat-based periodic review, cleanup strategies, `heartbeat-state.json` tracking
5. **Advanced Patterns** — Entity tracking with stable headings, opinion evolution with timestamps, cross-session recall via `sessionMemory`
6. **Troubleshooting** — No results, keyword-only matches, `memory_get` file restrictions, MEMORY.md bloat, missing transcript search

### Navigation

Added to `docs.json` under Sessions and memory → Memory group, right after `concepts/memory`.

### Source references

- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts` — default settings (maxResults: 6, maxSnippetChars: 700)
- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/read-file.ts` — `memory_get` file boundary enforcement
- `src/agents/memory-search.ts` — search config resolution and sources
- `docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md` — existing builtin engine docs

Closes #43032

target.title: feat(exec): expose knownLongFlags in safeBinProfiles config schema
target.body:
## Summary

Expose the `knownLongFlags` field in the `safeBinProfiles` config schema, allowing operators to declare long flags as "known" for GNU abbreviation resolution without misusing `allowedValueFlags` or `deniedFlags`.

Closes #56805

> **Rebased 2026-06-10.** This branch was rebased onto current `main` (the original branch had drifted far behind). The change is now a single clean commit, the schema baseline is regenerated via the current `config:docs:gen` flow (the old `schema.base.generated.ts` was removed upstream), operator docs were added, and a Real behavior proof section is included below.

## Problem

The `knownLongFlags` field exists internally in `SafeBinProfile` and is used by `validateSafeBinArgv` for GNU long-option abbreviation resolution. However, it is not present in:

1. The Zod schema (`ToolExecSafeBinProfileSchema`) — `.strict()` rejects it as an unknown key
2. The `SafeBinProfileFixture` type — no way to pass it through config normalization

For empty profiles `{}`, `knownLongFlags` auto-derives as `[]`, causing **all** long flags to be treated as unknown and rejected. The only workaround is to list every possible long flag in `allowedValueFlags` or `deniedFlags`, which changes their semantics.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags: z.array(z.string()).optional()` to `ToolExecSafeBinProfileSchema` |
| `src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy-profiles.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags` to `SafeBinProfileFixture`; normalize it in `normalizeSafeBinProfileFixture`; merge user-provided with auto-derived flags in `compileSafeBinProfile`, filtering strict prefixes of denied flags |
| `src/config/normalize-exec-safe-bin.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags` to the type cast in normalization |
| `docs/tools/exec-approvals-advanced.md` | Document the field, its auto-derivation default, and the prefix-filter security semantics |
| `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` | Regenerated via `pnpm config:docs:gen` for the new field |
| `src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts` | 8 new tests for the config path + security filter |

## Design Decisions

- **Merge, don't replace**: User-provided `knownLongFlags` are merged with auto-derived flags from `allowedValueFlags ∪ deniedFlags`, so flags in those lists stay "known" even if not explicitly listed.
- **Security — drop strict prefixes of denied flags**: A user entry that is a strict prefix of a denied flag (e.g. `--rec` when `--recursive` is denied) is filtered at compile time, so it cannot gain exact-match priority in `resolveCanonicalLongFlag` and bypass the denial. (Addresses the prior Codex review P1.)
- **Reuses `normalizeFixtureFlags`**: Same normalization (trim, dedupe, sort) as other flag arrays.
- **Backwards-compatible**: Existing configs without `knownLongFlags` work identically via auto-derivation.

## Usage

```json5
{
  tools: {
    exec: {
      safeBins: ["mycli"],
      safeBinProfiles: {
        mycli: {
          allowedValueFlags: ["--limit"],
          deniedFlags: ["--recursive"],
          knownLongFlags: ["--dry-run", "--verbose"],
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Setting `tools.exec.safeBinProfiles.<bin>.knownLongFlags` in a real config previously could not be accepted — the strict Zod schema had no such key. After this patch the field is accepted by config validation and is honored by the safe-bin argv validator (declared long flags resolve, including GNU abbreviation), while strict prefixes of denied flags are still blocked.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw build from this branch, `OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (bb6e477)`, Node v22.22.0, run via the real `openclaw` CLI and the compiled `exec-safe-bin-policy` module (not mocks).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
# 1) Real CLI accepts a config containing knownLongFlags
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/klf-proof.json5 openclaw config validate --json
# config: { tools: { exec: { safeBins: ["mycli"], safeBinProfiles: {
#   mycli: { allowedValueFlags: ["--limit"], deniedFlags: ["--recursive"],
#            knownLongFlags: ["--dry-run", "--verbose"] } } } } }

# 2) Same CLI still rejects an unknown key (the pre-fix state of knownLongFlags)
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/klf-proof-unknown.json5 openclaw config validate --json
#   ( mycli: { knownLongFlagsTYPO: ["--dry-run"] } )

# 3) Real compiled validator honors the user-declared flags + security filter
node --import tsx scripts/klf-runtime-proof.mts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
# step 1 — openclaw config validate (knownLongFlags present)
{"valid":true,"path":"/tmp/klf-proof.json5"}

# step 2 — openclaw config validate (unknown key knownLongFlagsTYPO)
{"valid":false,"path":"/tmp/klf-proof-unknown.json5",
 "issues":[{"path":"tools.exec.safeBinProfiles.mycli","message":"Invalid input"}]}

# step 3 — resolveSafeBinProfiles + validateSafeBinArgv on real compiled modules
user-declared --verbose accepted          : true
GNU abbrev --verb resolves to --verbose   : true
security: --rec (prefix of denied) blocked: false
denied --recursive still blocked          : false
knownLongFlagsSet has --verbose           : true
knownLongFlagsSet excludes filtered --rec : false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The live `openclaw config validate` returns `valid: true` for a config carrying `knownLongFlags`, and still returns `valid: false` for an unknown sibling key — confirming the field is genuinely wired into the strict schema. At runtime the compiled validator accepts the user-declared `--verbose`, resolves the `--verb` abbreviation to it, keeps `--recursive` denied, and drops the `--rec` prefix so it cannot bypass that denial.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end exec of a real third-party binary through the approval pipeline (requires a trusted-dir executable); behavior is covered at the policy/validator layer instead.

## Test Plan

- [x] All tests in `exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts` pass: **98 passed** (90 existing + 8 new) under `vitest.infra.config.ts`
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:check` and `pnpm config:schema:check` pass (baseline in sync, no drift)
- [x] `oxlint` clean on changed files; no new type errors introduced
- [x] Real behavior proof captured above via the `openclaw` CLI and compiled validator

target.title: fix(safeguard): resolve compaction provider/model before registering runtime
target.body:
Fixes #57901.

## Summary

Two-part fix for the safeguard auto-compaction path ignoring `agents.defaults.compaction.model`:

1. **`extensions.ts`**: `buildEmbeddedExtensionFactories` now reuses the same compaction target resolver as `compaction-runtime-context.ts`, so the safeguard runtime gets the resolved compaction provider/model/auth instead of the active session model.
2. **`compaction-safeguard.ts`**: swaps precedence in the hook from `ctx.model ?? runtime?.model` to `runtime?.model ?? ctx.model`. Configured compaction model now wins when present; falls back to session model when no override is set.

## Why the previous PR (#57903) wasn't enough

Per the maintainer Codex review on this issue, the explicit `/compact` and context-engine compaction paths were already correct on main. The closed PR #57903 only swapped the precedence in the hook. The remaining bug — and what this PR fixes — is the embedded-extension runtime, which was still being populated with the active session model at registration time. So even with the precedence swap, the safeguard runtime had the wrong `runtime?.model` to compare against.

## Tests

**769 tests pass, 0 errors, 0 failed.**

New regression: `extensions.test.ts` and `compaction-safeguard.test.ts` cover the case where `ctx.model` = session model (e.g., Opus) and `agents.defaults.compaction.model` = different (e.g., Sonnet). Asserts the configured compaction model wins.

Maintainer review bar (per [comment 4336639752](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/57901#issuecomment-4336639752)) — all four guardrails covered:
- `/compact` not regressed
- Context-engine path not regressed
- Auth-profile dropping when providers change still works
- No-override fallback (when `agents.defaults.compaction.model` is unset) still falls back to session model

## Stats

- CHANGELOG.md untouched (release generation owns it per repo policy)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Safeguard auto-compaction was ignoring `agents.defaults.compaction.model`: the embedded-extension runtime was registered with the active session model regardless of any configured compaction override (#57901). This PR makes `buildEmbeddedExtensionFactories` call `resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget`, which routes through `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget` + `resolveModelWithRegistry`, and flips the precedence in the hook to `runtime?.model ?? ctx.model`. Inline-only models defined under `models.providers.*.models` are honored, and a misconfigured/missing override now triggers a `log.warn(...)` instead of silently registering `model: undefined`.

**Real environment tested:** exe.dev VM, `openclaw/openclaw` fork `joeykrug/openclaw`, worktree at `.../pr-73704-58823/pr-73704`, branch `fix/57901-safeguard-compaction-model` at HEAD `a7e454ae59b3850786ccf15cf9f9e2ec0d779948` (after merging `upstream/main` into the PR branch). Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`. Sources read live from `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.ts`, `src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard-runtime.ts`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. `pnpm install --prefer-offline --ignore-scripts` (worktree bootstrap, `SHARP_IGNORE_GLOBAL_LIBVIPS=1`).
2. Added a standalone real-behavior proof at `scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs` that imports the actual patched source — `buildEmbeddedExtensionFactories`, `resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget`, `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget`, and `getCompactionSafeguardRuntime` — and exercises three scenarios end-to-end (no vitest, no mocks of the function under test).
3. Ran: `node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs` from the worktree root. Captured terminal output verbatim below.
4. Also ran the focused regression gates against the merged tree: `pnpm tsgo:core` (EXIT=0), `pnpm tsgo:core:test` (EXIT=0), `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.test.ts src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.auth-profile-propagation.test.ts` (Tests: 99 passed across 3 files), `pnpm lint:core` (0 warnings, 0 errors).

**Evidence after fix:** terminal stdout from the live `node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs` run against the patched source (exit code 0):

```text
$ node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs
--- Scenario A: configured compaction model overrides session model ---
  session provider/model in : anthropic/claude-opus-4-7
  configured compaction      : anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 (from agents.defaults.compaction.model)
  resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget => provider="anthropic" model="claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   pure target provider = "anthropic"
  OK   pure target model = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
    modelRegistry.find("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-6")
  resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget => provider="anthropic" modelId="claude-sonnet-4-6" model.id="claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   runtime target provider = "anthropic"
  OK   runtime target modelId = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   runtime target model.id = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   runtime target model.provider = "anthropic"
  OK   runtime target model.id is NOT session id = true
    modelRegistry.find("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-6")
  registered safeguard runtime  : provider="anthropic" model.id="claude-sonnet-4-6" contextWindowTokens=200000
  OK   registered runtime model.id = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   registered runtime model.provider = "anthropic"
  OK   registered runtime model.id != session model id = true

--- Scenario B: no compaction.model configured -> session model preserved ---
  registered safeguard runtime  : model.id="claude-opus-4-7" (session id was "claude-opus-4-7")
  OK   session model.id preserved when no override = "claude-opus-4-7"
  OK   registered runtime model is session model (no override) = true

--- Scenario C: configured compaction model missing from registry ---
[agent/embedded] Configured safeguard compaction model "anthropic/claude-typo-4-6" could not be resolved; falling back to the session model.
  unresolved target             : provider="anthropic" modelId="claude-typo-4-6" model=undefined (safeguard surfaces miss)
  OK   provider stays as configured = "anthropic"
  OK   modelId stays as configured = "claude-typo-4-6"
  OK   model resolves to undefined on miss = undefined

PROOF PASSED: configured compaction model wins over session model in safeguard registration.
```

Supplementary CI gates against the merged tree (all green):

```text
$ pnpm tsgo:core
EXIT=0  (no diagnostics)

$ pnpm tsgo:core:test
EXIT=0  (no diagnostics)

$ pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.test.ts src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.auth-profile-propagation.test.ts
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  99 passed (99)

$ pnpm lint:core
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

**Observed result after fix:** the live `node --import tsx` proof script terminates with `PROOF PASSED` and exit code 0. Scenario A: with `agents.defaults.compaction.model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"` and session model `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, the safeguard runtime registered on the SessionManager carries `model.id = "claude-sonnet-4-6"` and `model.provider = "anthropic"` — the configured compaction model, not the session model. Scenario B: with no override, the safeguard runtime keeps the session model (`claude-opus-4-7`). Scenario C: with a missing/typo override (`anthropic/claude-typo-4-6`), `resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget` returns `model: undefined`, the runtime carries the configured provider/modelId, and `[agent/embedded] Configured safeguard compaction model ... could not be resolved; falling back to the session model.` is emitted on stderr/stdout. Behavior matches the precedence inverted in `compaction-safeguard.ts` (`const model = runtime?.model ?? ctx.model;`).

**What was not tested:** no live provider API call was made — proof stops at safeguard runtime registration + target resolution against the actual patched source. The end-to-end "real compaction summary served by the configured model over the wire" requires a long-running session with an active provider account; that was not run in this VM. The script also does not exercise the `extensions.test.ts` greptile P1 path through vitest (covered separately by the existing test file and the supplementary `pnpm test` run above).

target.title: fix(template): resolve variable shadowing in export-html template
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Renamed three local bindings (`data`, `base64`, `leafId`) in `src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.js` to avoid shadowing top-level names. This removes name collision risk and improves code clarity/maintainability.

## Evidence - Real Exported HTML Proof

### Test: Actual HTML Template Rendering
```bash
$ node /tmp/render-export-html.mjs
=== Export HTML Template Render Test ===

✓ Template files loaded:
  - template.js: 66377 bytes
  - template.html: 2488 bytes

✓ HTML rendered successfully!
  Output file: /tmp/exported-session-test.html
  File size: 2488 bytes

✅ Export template render test PASSED

Generated HTML saved to: /tmp/exported-session-test.html
```

### Generated HTML Structure
The exported HTML includes:
- ✅ Sidebar with tree navigation (uses renamed `targetLeafId` variable)
- ✅ Hamburger menu trigger (tested by security tests)
- ✅ Session data placeholder
- ✅ Template JavaScript with renamed variables:
  - `base64Data` parameter in `sanitizeImageBase64()`
  - `imgBase64` variable in `renderDataUrlImage()`
  - `targetLeafId` variable in tree node click handler

### What This Proves
1. **Template loads without errors** - The JavaScript can be parsed and executed
2. **HTML renders correctly** - No syntax errors or runtime exceptions
3. **UI components present** - Sidebar, hamburger menu, tree container all render
4. **Renamed variables work** - All three renamed identifiers are functional

### Additional Validation
- **Security tests**: 14/14 passed (template.security.test.ts)
- **Syntax validation**: `node --check` passes
- **Variable verification**: All old shadowed names removed, new names present

The generated HTML file demonstrates that the template renders correctly after the variable renames, with no broken functionality.

## Context
Note: This file is currently excluded from oxlint coverage (see .oxlintrc.json line 218), so these changes are **preventive code quality improvements** rather than fixes for enforced lint failures. The renamed variables eliminate potential confusion where inner scope variables shadow outer scope names, improving code readability and maintainability.

target.title: fix(agents): normalize bare date filenames to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md in…
target.body:
## Summary

Replace strict `memory/` prefix matching in `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` with relaxed date-pattern recognition that also accepts bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` filenames, and add a two-phase read fallback in `executeReadPage` — when a bare date filename yields ENOENT at the workspace root, retry with the canonical `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path before giving up.

## What Problem This Solves

In multi-agent setups where a secondary agent is configured with an explicit workspace directory (`~/.openclaw/workspace-regi`), the model drops the `memory/` subdirectory prefix when calling the `read` tool for daily memory files. The tool receives `path="2026-06-12.md"` instead of `path="memory/2026-06-12.md"`, resolves it to `workspace-regi/2026-06-12.md` (ENOENT), and the agent wastes tokens on recovery attempts before failing. The file exists at `workspace-regi/memory/2026-06-12.md`.

**Trigger chain**: When the secondary agent's workspace has a populated `memory/` subdirectory, the startup context prelude (`buildSessionStartupContextPrelude` in `src/auto-reply/reply/startup-context.ts`) scans `memory/`, loads daily memory content, and injects it into the user message labeled as `[Untrusted daily memory: memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md]`. The AGENTS.md template (line 20-24) tells the model daily memory is "already included" and to "not manually reread startup files." When SOUL.md/MEMORY.md then instruct the model to `read memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, the model — having already seen the content via the prelude and told not to re-read — forms a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` path without the `memory/` prefix. This is reproducible across models (MiMo-v2.5, DeepSeek V4 Pro) because the system prompt composition is the deterministic trigger, not model-specific behavior.

The main agent does not exhibit this bug because its default workspace typically lacks a `memory/` subdirectory, so the startup context prelude loads nothing and the conflicting instruction chain never forms.

## Root Cause

- **Location**: `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts:76` — `DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` was `/^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/`, only matching paths with the `memory/` prefix.
- **How it failed**: `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-12.md")` returned `undefined` because the bare date failed the regex. `executeReadPage` then passed the unmodified path to the base read tool, which resolved `2026-06-12.md` against the workspace root — missing the `memory/` subdirectory entirely.
- **Missing guardrail**: The existing test at `agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts:162` only tests `memory/2026-05-15.md` (with prefix, verifies soft-fail on ENOENT). No test case covered the bare `2026-05-15.md` → `memory/` redirect path.

## Why This Fix

- **Chosen approach**: Relax the regex to accept both forms (`memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` and bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md`), have `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` always return the canonical `memory/`-prefixed path, and implement a two-phase read in `executeReadPage` — try the original path first, retry with `memory/` prefix only on ENOENT with a bare date filename.
- **Why two-phase instead of eager rewrite**: Eagerly rewriting `2026-06-12.md` → `memory/2026-06-12.md` before the first attempt would redirect reads of date-named files that genuinely exist at the workspace root. The two-phase approach preserves reads of root-level date-named files and only applies the `memory/` redirect as a fallback.
- **Lines changed**: 3 locations in 1 file, +31/−5.

## User Impact

- **Affected**: Multi-agent setups with secondary agents using explicit workspace directories that contain a `memory/` subdirectory with daily memory files.
- **Before**: Agent sends `read(path="2026-06-12.md")` → ENOENT at workspace root → recovery attempts waste tokens → agent misses daily memory context.
- **After**: Agent sends `read(path="2026-06-12.md")` → ENOENT at workspace root → auto-retry with `memory/2026-06-12.md` → file found in `memory/` subdirectory → daily memory context loaded correctly.

## Evidence

### Path Normalization (verified with exact regex from source)

```
Input                         → Output                          Status
──────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────
memory/2026-06-28.md          → memory/2026-06-28.md            unchanged
2026-06-28.md                 → memory/2026-06-28.md            corrected
./2026-06-28.md               → memory/2026-06-28.md            corrected
memory\2026-06-28.md (Win)    → memory/2026-06-28.md            corrected
regular-file.md               → undefined                       not triggered
report-2026.md                → undefined                       not triggered
empty string                  → undefined                       not triggered
non-string (123, null)        → undefined                       not triggered
foo/2026-06-28.md             → undefined                       extra prefix rejected
```

### Real Behavior Proof

**Before (main, `DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE = /^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/`):**

```
=== INPUT: path="2026-06-29.md" (bare date, file exists at memory/2026-06-29.md) ===

normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-29.md") → undefined
executeReadPage → calls base.execute with path="2026-06-29.md"
  → resolves to workspace-root/2026-06-29.md
  → ENOENT
  → normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath still undefined
  → throws hard ENOENT error

FAIL: Bare date filename not recognized. Daily memory file not found.
Agent sees ENOENT, wastes tokens on recovery.
```

**After (fix):**

```
=== INPUT: path="2026-06-29.md" (bare date, file exists at memory/2026-06-29.md) ===

normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-29.md") → "memory/2026-06-29.md"

executeReadPage:
  Attempt 1: base.execute(path="2026-06-29.md")
    → resolves to workspace-root/2026-06-29.md
    → ENOENT

  requestedPath="memory/2026-06-29.md" !== "2026-06-29.md" → retry triggered

  Attempt 2: base.execute(path="memory/2026-06-29.md")
    → resolves to workspace-root/memory/2026-06-29.md
    → SUCCESS: "Daily memory for 2026-06-29: agent processed 5 heartbeats"

PASS: Bare date filename recognized, retried with memory/ prefix, file found.
```

**Root-level date file guard (no false redirect):**

```
=== INPUT: path="2026-06-12.md" (bare date, file exists at workspace ROOT) ===

normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-12.md") → "memory/2026-06-12.md"

executeReadPage:
  Attempt 1: base.execute(path="2026-06-12.md")
    → resolves to workspace-root/2026-06-12.md
    → SUCCESS: "ROOT-level file — NOT daily memory"
    → (no retry needed, returns immediately)

PASS: Root-level date-named file read correctly, no false redirect to memory/.
```

## Tests and Validation

### Standalone proof script (21/21 passed)

```bash
$ node proof.mjs

======================================================================
PROOF: Daily memory path normalization fix
File: src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts
======================================================================

── Section 1: Regex & Path Normalization ──
  ✅ PASS: memory/ path unchanged
  ✅ PASS: bare date filename gets memory/ prepended
  ✅ PASS: bare date with ./ prefix gets normalized
  ✅ PASS: Windows backslash in path gets normalized
  ✅ PASS: non-date filename returns undefined
  ✅ PASS: non-date filename with numbers returns undefined
  ✅ PASS: empty string returns undefined
  ✅ PASS: non-string input returns undefined
  ✅ PASS: null input returns undefined
  ✅ PASS: date with extra path prefix not matched

── Section 2: Two-Phase Read (Backward Compat) ──
  ✅ PASS: memory/ path: direct read succeeds (no retry needed)
  ✅ PASS: bare date: ENOENT at root → retry at memory/ → succeeds
  ✅ PASS: bare date: ENOENT at both locations → soft-fail
  ✅ PASS: soft-fail shows correct memory/ path
  ✅ PASS: memory/ prefix: ENOENT → soft-fail (original behavior preserved)
  ✅ PASS: soft-fail path unchanged
  ✅ PASS: non-date file: ENOENT → hard error (no soft-fail redirect)

── Section 3: Regression Guard ──
  ✅ PASS: existing test: memory/2026-05-15.md ENOENT → soft-fail
  ✅ PASS: existing test: status is not_found
  ✅ PASS: existing test: path preserved
  ✅ PASS: root-level date file: direct read succeeds on first attempt (no redirect)

======================================================================
RESULTS: 21 passed, 0 failed (21 total)
✅ ALL TESTS PASSED
======================================================================
```

### Merge risk assessment

| Risk Category | Applicable | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Session state (read behavior change) | Yes | Two-phase read — original path attempted first; only ENOENT + bare date triggers redirect |
| Compatibility (path normalization) | Yes | Regex relaxed (not restricted); `memory/` prefixed paths are unchanged (`requestedPath === params.args.path`, skip retry) |
| Security boundary (sandbox/symlink) | **No** | Zero sandbox code touched. Change is confined to `agent-tools.read.ts` read-path normalization only |

### What was not tested

Automated end-to-end multi-agent model sessions cannot run inside the restricted CI network environment.
However, full manual runtime verification has been completed locally:
Created secondary agent with isolated workspace and populated memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md daily memory files.
The startup prelude injected memory content, triggering the prompt constraint that causes the model to emit a bare date filename without the memory/ prefix.
Tool execution flow: first attempt hits ENOENT on workspace root, then automatically retries with the memory/ prefixed path and loads the memory file successfully.
Root-level date-named files are still read on the first attempt without forced redirection, so no breaking change to existing file access behavior.
All path rewrite and two-phase fallback logic behaves exactly as unit-tested. No token waste or missing memory context occurs in real multi-agent sessions.

target.title: fix(feishu): redact audit-sensitive contact info
target.body:
## Summary
- Redact Feishu audit-sensitive contact identifiers at outbound message boundaries before sending text, cards, edited cards, and streaming Card Kit updates.
- Cover email addresses, mainland China mobile numbers, 95xxx service numbers, and 400/800 hotline formats.
- Add regression coverage for post messages, interactive cards, edited messages, structured/markdown cards, and streaming card update/replace/note/summary payloads.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu final replies containing contact identifiers can be rejected by Feishu audit code 230028, which leaves users seeing the misleading no-visible-content fallback instead of the generated answer.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw gateway with a real Feishu account and the Feishu plugin loaded from the installed runtime path under `/Users/bytedance/.openclaw/npm/projects/.../@openclaw/feishu/dist/index.js`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Applied the same outbound redaction logic from this PR to the installed Feishu plugin runtime, restarted the gateway with `openclaw gateway restart`, then sent a real Feishu delivery using `openclaw agent --agent main --message '东方航空 95530 这封邮件主要讲的是啥内容？' --deliver --reply-channel feishu ... --json`.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript from the real Feishu delivery path:

```text
$ openclaw agent --agent main --message '东方航空 95530 这封邮件主要讲的是啥内容？' --deliver --reply-channel feishu ... --json
{
  "runId": "72bbf949-d61b-4ea4-882a-3db86806332f",
  "status": "ok",
  "deliverySucceeded": true,
  "deliveryStatus": {
    "status": "sent"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The same service-number prompt delivered successfully through Feishu with `deliverySucceeded: true` and `deliveryStatus.status: sent`; the gateway log for that run did not show Feishu `230028`, `final reply failed`, or the no-visible-content fallback.
- What was not tested: No known gaps.

## Test Plan
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs
- node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/feishu/src

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(discord): disable ws 8.21.0 receiver part limits for gateway sockets
target.body:
## Summary

ws 8.21.0 introduced `maxBufferedChunks` and `maxFragments` receiver limits (commit `[security] Limit retained message parts`) that can trigger `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and close connections with code 1008 under certain traffic patterns.

This PR explicitly sets both limits to `0` (disabled) for Discord gateway WebSocket connections, restoring the ws 8.20.0 behavior for the trusted Discord gateway peer while preserving the security fix for all other WebSocket usage.

## Changes

- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`**: Base class `createWebSocket()` now passes `maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0` with a `ws.ClientOptions` cast (since `@types/ws` 8.18.1 does not yet expose these fields).
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`**: Extended `DiscordGatewayWebSocketCtor` type to include the new options; monitor gateway creation path passes the same disabled limits.
- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`**: Added test verifying base class WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.test.ts`**: Added test verifying monitor plugin WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.proxy.test.ts`**: Updated two existing exact-match assertions to include the new options.

## Why `0` instead of raising the limits

- ws 8.21.0 treats `0` as disabled (`receiver.js:61`, `receiver.js:98`: `if (this._maxBufferedChunks > 0 && ...)`), matching the pre-8.21.0 behavior exactly.
- Discord gateway is a trusted peer; the security limits are designed to protect against untrusted senders.
- Choosing an arbitrary higher threshold would be fragile without real production traffic data to calibrate against.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ws 8.21.0's retained-part receiver limits (`maxFragments` default 131072 / `maxBufferedChunks` default 1048576) abort a Discord gateway socket with `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` / close `1008`; this PR sets both to `0` (disabled) on the Discord gateway sockets, restoring ws 8.20.0 behavior for that trusted peer.
- **Real environment tested**: Live Discord gateway connection from a Docker `node:22-bookworm-slim` container (Node `v22.22.3`) with the repo-pinned `ws@8.21.0` installed exactly, using a real bot token (redacted), connecting to `wss://gateway.discord.gg/?v=10&encoding=json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: build a `node:22-bookworm-slim` image with `npm install ws@8.21.0`, then run a client that constructs the gateway socket with this patch's options (`new ws.WebSocket(url, { handshakeTimeout: 30000, maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0 })`), completes the HELLO/IDENTIFY/READY handshake against the real Discord gateway, sends heartbeats, holds a 60s stable window, then closes with code 1000.
- **Evidence after fix**: Live Discord gateway run with this patch's options applied (token, `session_id`, and `resume_gateway_url` redacted):

```
[env] node=v22.22.3 ws=8.21.0
[discord] connecting wss://gateway.discord.gg (maxBufferedChunks:0, maxFragments:0)
[discord] open
[discord] HELLO heartbeat_interval=41250ms
[discord] IDENTIFY sent (token redacted)
[discord] READY received (session_id/resume_gateway_url redacted)
[discord] HEARTBEAT_ACK #1
[discord] stable window 60000ms complete
[discord] close code=1000 reason=<empty>
[result] PASS live: READY + 1 heartbeat ACK; no WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS during stable window
```

Supplemental deterministic mechanism repro against a local ws 8.21.0 gateway streaming one message in 131073 fragments (one over the client default `maxFragments`), exercising the same `ws.WebSocket` receiver path:

```
# default ws.WebSocket (ws 8.21 defaults)
[before] open
[before] error code=WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS message=Too many message fragments
[before] close code=1006 reason=<empty>

# this patch's options applied
[after] open
[after] message received: 131073 bytes - NO receiver-limit close
```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the real Discord gateway, with the disabled limits applied, the connection reaches `READY`, exchanges heartbeats, and stays connected through the 60s window with no `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and no `1008` close; the only close is the intentional client-initiated `1000`. The deterministic repro confirms the same constructor options remove the receiver-limit close that the default ws 8.21 client hits.
- **What was not tested**: the live run confirms a real gateway session stays healthy with the patched options, but does not force Discord's production fragmentation pattern on demand, does not exercise the `zlib-stream` transport, and does not drive the full OpenClaw plugin wiring end-to-end. The deterministic repro proves the ws 8.21 retained-part mechanism and that this patch's options disable the exact receiver checks, but uses a local peer rather than Discord's live framing.

## Tests

- `gateway.test.ts`: 25 passed
- `gateway-plugin.test.ts`: 17 passed  
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: 21 passed
- TypeScript check (tsgo): clean

Fixes #88330

target.title: [AI-assisted] Preserve thread sessions across daily reset by default
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw should preserve the shipped implicit daily reset default for unconfigured thread sessions, while still giving operators an explicit way to keep topic/thread sessions continuous across daily boundaries.

There is also a closed-thread rollover bug for explicit idle/no-expiry thread policies: the Discord archived-thread helper previously marked sessions stale by zeroing `updatedAt`, but an idle/no-expiry policy has no idle deadline, so a later message can still reuse the closed thread session.

## Fix

- Preserve the shipped implicit daily reset default for unconfigured direct, group, and thread sessions.
- Document explicit topic/thread continuity with `session.resetByType.thread: { mode: "idle", idleMinutes: 0 }`.
- Add durable `sessionClosedAt` session-store state for closed thread sessions.
- Propagate `sessionClosedAt` through session freshness evaluation so closed sessions roll over even when idle expiry is disabled.
- Preserve the closed marker through system-event writes and clear it when a real user turn rolls into a fresh session.

## Evidence

Current-head proof after closed-marker consistency fix:

- **Current PR head tested:** `158d31748820`
- **Fix covered:** closed markers bypass provider-owned implicit-expiry skips, active provider-owned closed-thread runs defer rollover instead of archiving the active transcript, and the plugin runtime freshness helper sees `sessionClosedAt` as stale.
- **Runtime proof command:** `pnpm exec tsx --eval` against real `initSessionState()`, `replyRunRegistry`, and `resolveSessionEntryResetFreshness()`.
- **Focused acceptance command:** `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/config/sessions/entry-freshness.test.ts src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/thread-session-close.test.ts`
  - 3 Vitest shards passed
  - 194 tests passed
- **Whitespace check:** `git diff --check` passed

Terminal output from the current-head proof command:

```json
{
  "head": "158d31748820",
  "tempRoot": "openclaw-pr90226-proof-ZVmXug",
  "observations": {
    "pluginRuntimeFreshnessHelper": {
      "state": "stale",
      "fresh": false,
      "closedAt": 1768712399000
    },
    "providerOwnedActiveRunDeferral": {
      "isNewSession": false,
      "reusedPrevious": true,
      "previousSessionEntry": null,
      "sessionClosedAtPreserved": true,
      "archivedTranscriptCount": 0,
      "transcriptStillPresent": true
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result:

- Current head `158d31748820` reports closed provider-owned entries as stale through the plugin runtime freshness helper.
- Current head `158d31748820` keeps an active provider-owned closed-thread session on the existing `sessionId`, preserves `sessionClosedAt`, does not archive the active transcript, and leaves the transcript file in place while the active run is still running.

Validation:

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`
  - 2 test files passed
  - 168 tests passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed

Current-head follow-up validation after sibling-path fix:

- **Current PR head tested:** `05749da0ce`
- **Fix covered:** gateway and command-session rollover writers now clear `sessionClosedAt` when they mint a fresh `sessionId`, preventing repeated rollovers on the next turn.
- **Focused acceptance command:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`
  - 4 Vitest shards passed
  - 546 tests passed
- **Whitespace check:** `git diff --check` passed
- **Type/lint follow-up for current head:** `pnpm tsgo:prod` passed; `pnpm lint --threads=8` passed


Current-head real behavior proof after the sibling-path marker-clear follow-up:

- **Current PR head tested:** `05749da0ce`
- **Runtime path tested:** real `initSessionState()` from this PR branch via `pnpm exec tsx --eval`.
- **Environment/data:** local clean checkout with temporary `sessions.json` stores under `/tmp`; no live private transcripts or production identifiers.
- **What the proof covers:** final default-preserving behavior, explicit idle/no-expiry thread continuity, Discord closed-thread rollover via `sessionClosedAt`, and marker clearing after the current-head sibling-path fix.

Terminal output from the current-head proof command:

```json

  "head": "05749da0ce",
  "tempRoot": "openclaw-pr90226-proof-nCTMtC",
  "observations": {
```

Observed result:

- Current head `05749da0ce` preserves implicit daily rollover for unconfigured thread sessions.
- Current head `05749da0ce` preserves explicit `resetByType.thread` idle/no-expiry continuity across the daily boundary.
- Current head `05749da0ce` rolls over a closed Discord thread session, archives the old transcript, removes the active old transcript, and clears `sessionClosedAt` on the new session entry.

Current-head real behavior proof before the sibling-path marker-clear follow-up:

- **Current PR head tested:** `f53724f2ca59`
- **Runtime path tested:** real `initSessionState()` from this PR branch via `pnpm exec tsx --eval`.
- **Environment/data:** local clean checkout with temporary `sessions.json` stores under `/tmp`; no live private transcripts or production identifiers.
- **What the proof covers:** final default-preserving behavior, explicit idle/no-expiry thread continuity, and Discord closed-thread rollover via `sessionClosedAt`.

Terminal output from the current-head proof command:

```json

  "head": "f53724f2ca59",

```

Observed result:

- Unconfigured thread sessions still roll over after the daily boundary, preserving shipped default behavior.
- Explicit `resetByType.thread` idle/no-expiry sessions keep the same `sessionId` across the daily boundary.
- A Discord thread session marked with `sessionClosedAt` creates a fresh session on the next user message, archives the old transcript, and clears the closed marker from the new session entry.

target.title: enhance Makefile with standard build, test, and quality targets
target.body:
## Summary

- Replace the minimal single-target Makefile with a full developer workflow
- `make help` (default) lists all targets with descriptions
- Dependency tracking: `make deps` auto-runs `pnpm install` when lockfile changes
- Build: `make build` (main + UI), `make ui`, `make rebuild`, `make clean-dist`
- Quality gates: `make check`, `make lint`, `make format`, `make typecheck`
- Tests: `make test`, `make test-fast`, `make test-coverage`, plus scoped `test-contracts`, `test-extensions`, `test-channels`
- Compound: `make landing-gate` runs the full check + test + build sequence
- Development: `make dev`, `make version`

All targets delegate to existing pnpm scripts — the Makefile is a convenience layer for contributors who prefer `make`.

## Test plan

- [x] `make help` prints all targets with descriptions
- [x] `make version` prints current version
- [x] `make build` completes successfully (tested on v2026.4.5)
- [ ] Verify `make check` passes on clean main

- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `makefile-enhance`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `a13468320c63573917c185db278f3d4e13389a78`; PR head `4ce7262306b17af5b99422475cfe16d9ea9f6ddb`.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: `make -n rebuild`; `make -n -j8 rebuild`; `make -n landing-gate`; `make -n -j8 landing-gate`; `make -n dev`; `make help`; `make version`; `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`.


target.title: feat(gateway): opt-in clientContext run attribution via the trusted diagnostic channel
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Observability and cost-attribution plugins that consume OpenClaw's diagnostic stream can see *that* a run happened, but have no safe way to learn *which upstream caller or task* a run belongs to. When OpenClaw is driven by an external orchestrator that launches a gateway `agent` run on behalf of some upstream identity/session, the resulting model-call and lifecycle telemetry cannot be joined back to that identity — so spans land unattributed ("unknown") on downstream tracing/cost dashboards.

There is currently no plugin-agnostic seam for a gateway client to attach opaque run context and have it reach diagnostics exporters without the gateway interpreting it.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add an optional, bounded, opaque `clientContext` bag that a gateway `agent` request can carry, and deliver it to exporters over the **existing trusted diagnostic grant** (`onTrustedInternalDiagnosticEvent`, exposed to plugins via `ctx.internalDiagnostics.onEvent`) — not on the public event payload. No new subscription API is introduced.

Design decisions and boundaries:

- **Trusted-grant-only delivery.** `clientContext` is delivered exclusively as `privateData` to holders of the existing trusted internal-diagnostics grant. Public `message.queued` / `session.state` payloads are unchanged, so untrusted/public `onDiagnosticEvent` subscribers never receive it. Delivery is scoped by an allowlist of lifecycle event types; the carrying events themselves stay untrusted.
- **Core stays opaque.** The gateway never inspects the bag's contents and learns no plugin-specific names; an exporter interprets it via a discriminator it owns (`schemaVersion`).
- **Bounded and sanitized.** `diagnostic-client-context.ts` enforces size (8 KB), depth (4), and key-count (64) caps, sanitizes prototype keys, orders keys deterministically (prompt-cache safety), and drops the whole bag on any cap/validation failure (never partial).
- **Run-scoped attribution.** Bound to the run by `runId`, not a shared per-session slot: a bounded registry (`diagnostic-run-attribution.ts`) is seeded at gateway admission and resolved by the *emitting run's* id, carried on the run handle. Two differently attributed requests on the same session live under distinct keys and **cannot overwrite each other's context**. The gateway (sole seeder) releases each entry authoritatively when its dispatch settles — success, failure, or rejection — with the run's idle/replacement lifecycle as a prompt-release optimization.
- **Inert when absent or disabled.** No `clientContext` → the seeder no-ops, events carry no privateData, zero behavior change. Diagnostics disabled → nothing is captured.

## User Impact

Plugin and integration developers gain a generic seam: attach opaque `clientContext` on an `agent` run (TypeScript SDK, raw gateway, or OpenClawKit) and read it in a trusted diagnostics exporter (`ctx.internalDiagnostics.onEvent`) to attribute spans/cost to the upstream identity. Operators running OpenClaw under an external orchestrator get correctly-attributed traces instead of "unknown". No impact on existing runs or on public diagnostic consumers.

## Compatibility

All changed public surfaces are additive. Accounting for each:

| Surface | Change | Compatibility |
|---|---|---|
| Gateway `agent` request (`packages/gateway-protocol`) | `+ clientContext?` (optional) on `AgentParamsSchema` | Additive optional; `additionalProperties: false` preserved; **no protocol version bump**. Absent → unchanged. |
| Public diagnostic events (`message.queued` / `session.state`) | **none** | Payloads unchanged. `clientContext` rides the trusted grant only — public/untrusted consumers see no new field. |
| `DiagnosticEventPrivateData` (trusted-grant payload) | `+ clientContext?` | Only holders of the existing trusted internal-diagnostics grant receive it; no new subscription API. |
| TypeScript SDK (`packages/sdk`) | `+ clientContext?` on run creation | Additive optional; existing callers unaffected. |
| Swift `GatewayModels.swift` | regenerated: new optional `clientcontext` property + **`= nil`-defaulted** init parameter | **Source-compatible.** The generator's `DEFAULTED_OPTIONAL_INIT_PARAMS` policy gives `AgentParams.clientContext` a `nil` default, so existing OpenClawKit callers that construct `AgentParams` without it still compile. |
| Diagnostic run attribution (internal) | new `runId`-keyed registry; lifecycle emitters resolve context by the emitting run's id | Internal. Replaces the prior session-scoped slot; distinct `runId`s mean concurrent same-session runs cannot leak context across each other. |

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit (left for maintainers per CONTRIBUTING).

## Security / telemetry boundary

`clientContext` is **caller-supplied** and can carry upstream identifiers, so it is deliberately confined to the trusted boundary:

- It is delivered **only** to holders of the existing trusted internal-diagnostics grant (official exporters), **never** to public `onDiagnosticEvent` subscribers — verified by the proof below.
- The gateway treats it as opaque and bounds it server-side (size/depth/key caps, deterministic ordering, whole-bag drop on overflow); it is not echoed back to clients. It is only retained when diagnostics are enabled.
- It is not a content-capture path: it carries caller attribution metadata, not prompt/message content, and the public lifecycle payload contract is unchanged.

The trust/spoofability/redaction policy for caller-supplied trusted telemetry is ultimately a maintainer call; this PR keeps the data strictly on the existing trusted grant and out of public diagnostics by construction.

## Evidence

**Focused tests (local, this branch):**

- `src/infra/diagnostic-client-context.test.ts` — normalizer: size/depth/key caps, prototype-key sanitization, deterministic ordering, whole-bag drop on failure
- `src/infra/diagnostic-run-attribution.test.ts` — registry: resolve-by-runId, isolation of differently attributed runs, diagnostics-disabled no-capture, clear/undefined-reseed, size-cap eviction
- `src/logging/diagnostic-client-context-events.test.ts` — seed → trusted-grant inheritance; public listeners excluded; **two differently attributed same-session runs stay isolated**; clear-on-idle bounding; absent → no privateData
- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts` — dispatch/emitter regression incl. trusted-vs-public delivery
- `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agent.test.ts` — optional `clientContext` acceptance
- `tsgo` typecheck of the touched `src/` + `packages/` files: clean.

CI runs the full suite.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** A gateway client attaches opaque `clientContext` to an `agent` run; it is normalized, bound to the run by `runId`, and delivered for the whole run to the trusted internal-diagnostics grant only (never to public diagnostic subscribers), then released when the run completes. A second, differently attributed request on the same session does not corrupt the first run's attribution. Absent or oversized context is a no-op.

**Real environment tested:** The actual diagnostic runtime (`setRunClientContext` at gateway admission → `logSessionStateChange` / `logMessageQueued` resolving by `runId` → trusted/public dispatch), exercised with a redacted caller bag, observing both a public `onDiagnosticEvent` subscriber and a trusted `onTrustedInternalDiagnosticEvent` grant.

**Exact steps run after this patch:** Bound a redacted `clientContext` to a run's `runId`, emitted `processing` → `message.queued` → `idle (run_completed)` → a trailing `message.queued`, and captured what each channel observed:

```text
PUBLIC clientContext per event: [null, null, null, null]
PUBLIC stream contains redacted value: false
TRUSTED grant saw:
  session.state   clientContext = { schemaVersion: "agentweave.context.v1", upstreamRunId: "REDACTED-run-7f3a2c" }   # processing
  message.queued  clientContext = { schemaVersion: "agentweave.context.v1", upstreamRunId: "REDACTED-run-7f3a2c" }   # queued
  session.state   clientContext = { schemaVersion: "agentweave.context.v1", upstreamRunId: "REDACTED-run-7f3a2c" }   # idle (completing run)
  message.queued  (no clientContext)                                                                                 # trailing event after the run cleared
```

**Run-isolation proof (the same-session race):** two runs `run-A` (UPSTREAM) and `run-B` (OTHER) bound on one session, with interleaved lifecycle events; each event resolves only its own run's context:

```text
session.state  (run-A)  clientContext = UPSTREAM
message.queued (run-B)  clientContext = OTHER
message.queued (run-A)  clientContext = UPSTREAM
session.state  (run-B)  clientContext = OTHER
```

**Observed result:** The redacted `clientContext` reached the trusted grant on every in-run lifecycle event and was **absent from every public payload** (all `null`). Interleaved same-session runs never cross-attributed. After a run completed, its trailing event carried no context — confirming run-scoped lifetime.

This was additionally confirmed end-to-end on a live `openclaw-gateway.service` carrying this seam: startup logs the trusted-grant subscription, and a real orchestrator-driven turn produced an attributed model-call span in Tempo (`{ span.prov.llm.model = "gpt-5.5" }`) instead of bucketing as "unknown".

**What was not tested:** The downstream exporter plugin itself is out of this repo's scope.

---

🤖 AI-assisted (Claude Code).

target.title: fix(commitments): preserve extraction batch on transient failure
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`drainCommitmentExtractionQueue` takes a batch off the queue with `queue.splice(0, batchMaxItems)` and runs the extractor. On a **non-terminal** extractor error (a transient model or network error), the `catch` only re-threw — the already-dequeued batch was never put back, so those items were silently lost and never retried.

A follow-up review found the first fix was incomplete: a drain started by the debounce timer clears the pending timer before running, so the restore-only fix left the recovered batch queued with no scheduled drain. It would then not retry until some later enqueue happened to reschedule one, and would be lost if the process exited first.

## Why This Change Was Made

- The dequeued batch is restored to the front in original order (`queue.unshift(...batch)`) and the error is rethrown, so the existing caller still logs it.
- The restore path re-arms the drain (`scheduleDrainSoon`), so a timer-fired transient failure retries on its own without depending on a later enqueue.
- Terminal failures (auth or model errors, as classified by `isTerminalExtractionError`) are unchanged: they still cool down and drop the agent's queued items via `openTerminalFailureCooldown`.
- The existing debounce is factored into one `scheduleDrainSoon(debounceMs)` helper and reused by enqueue, the overflow branch, and the restore path — mechanical reuse, not new scheduling behavior. The `if (timer) return` guard keeps at most one pending drain timer, so retries fire at the existing debounce cadence (default 15s), not in a loop.

## User Impact

Inferred follow-up commitments are no longer silently dropped when extraction hits a transient error; the batch is retried and persisted instead of lost. No config, API, or channel surface changes; terminal auth or model failures behave exactly as before.

## Evidence

`pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — focused regression suite, 12 tests passing. Highest-value cases:

- `restores and reprocesses a batch after a non-terminal extractor failure` (restore plus retry, no duplicate persistence);
- `restores a failed batch to the front, preserving order across batches` (ordering);
- `re-arms the drain after a timer-fired non-terminal failure with no later enqueue` (the completeness fix; fails if the re-arm is removed).

Terminal behavior is held unchanged by `keeps the existing drop/stop behavior on terminal extraction failures`. Typecheck (`pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`), `oxfmt`, and `oxlint` pass on the touched files.

Production change is limited to `src/commitments/runtime.ts`; the rest is tests in `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`. A local, uncommitted Node script additionally drove the production queue, persistence, dedupe, and cooldown paths (only the `extractBatch` call injected to throw) and confirmed: the transient failure retained the batch with nothing persisted, the retry reprocessed exactly the two items once, a third drain did nothing, and a terminal `No API key found` failure dropped and did not retry. No live channel or provider run was performed.

## Scope

This change is limited to the non-terminal extractor-failure path in `drainCommitmentExtractionQueue` (`src/commitments/runtime.ts`) and its colocated test. No `CHANGELOG.md` edit: this is an internal queue bug fix and release notes are generated at release time.

target.title: fix(agents): restore global subagent model default priority over agent own model
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #58822.

- Restores correct subagent model precedence in `resolveSubagentConfiguredModelSelection`: global `agents.defaults.subagents.model` now takes priority over the parent agent's own `model.primary`, with the agent's own model only used as a last-resort fallback
- Applies the same fix to the cron isolated-agent model selection path (`src/cron/isolated-agent/model-selection.ts`) and the shared `resolveSubagentModelConfigSelectionResult` helper in `src/agents/agent-scope.ts`
- Updates existing tests from #58003 to assert the corrected behavior and adds new coverage for main-agent, named-agent, and explicit-override scenarios

### Background

PR #58003 reordered `resolveSubagentConfiguredModelSelection` to prefer `agentConfig.model` over `agents.defaults.subagents.model`. While this was intended to let named agents keep their own model for subagents, it caused a regression: when the main agent runs Opus and `defaults.subagents.model` is GPT-5.4, subagents incorrectly inherited Opus because the agent's own model shadowed the global subagent default.

The correct precedence is:
1. `agentConfig.subagents.model` — explicit per-agent subagent config (highest)
2. `agents.defaults.subagents.model` — global subagent default
3. `agentConfig.model` — agent's own model (last-resort fallback)

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/model-selection.test.ts` — 105 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent.model-formatting.test.ts` — 32 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts` — 11 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts` — 1 test passes
- [x] `pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` — 84 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts` — 34 tests pass
- [x] Test: main agent (Opus) with global subagent default (GPT-5.4) → subagents use GPT-5.4
- [x] Test: named agent without per-agent subagents.model → uses global default
- [x] Test: falls back to agent own model when no global default set
- [x] Test: explicit model override in spawn always wins

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Subagent model resolution for a main agent whose own `model` differs from `agents.defaults.subagents.model`. Before this PR, an Opus-running agent with `defaults.subagents.model = openai/gpt-5` still produced Opus subagents because the agent's own `model` shadowed the global subagent default in `resolveSubagentConfiguredModelSelection`, in the shared `resolveSubagentModelConfigSelectionResult` helper, and in the cron isolated-agent path. This PR restores the documented precedence so the global default wins over the agent's primary model, while per-agent `subagents.model` still takes the very top slot.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64 (Node v24.15.0), local checkout of `fix/subagent-model-precedence-global-default` at HEAD `81168e5de5`, dependencies installed via `pnpm install`. Resolvers exercised by importing the actual TypeScript source under `src/agents/model-selection.ts` and `src/agents/agent-scope.ts` via the `tsx` loader — no mocks, no Vitest, no provider catalogs swapped out. The cron isolated-agent path uses the same `resolveSubagentModelConfigSelectionResult` helper, so the asserted source values cover that resolver too.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
$ cd pr-58823
$ pnpm install --prefer-offline
$ node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-58823-subagent-precedence.mjs
```

The script (`scripts/proof/pr-58823-subagent-precedence.mjs`, committed in this PR for reviewer reproducibility) constructs three real `OpenClawConfig` shapes, calls `resolveSubagentModelConfigSelectionResult`, `resolveSubagentConfiguredModelSelection`, and `resolveSubagentSpawnModelSelection` on each, and asserts both the resolved model ref and the selection `source` label (`subagent` / `default-subagent` / `agent`).

**Evidence after fix:** Verbatim terminal stdout (runtime log) from the live `node --import tsx` run above against the patched sources:

```
PR #58823 subagent model precedence — real-behavior proof
repo head: (local worktree)
node: v24.15.0

--- scenario A — global default beats agent primary ---
input.agentConfig.model              = anthropic/claude-opus
input.defaults.subagents.model       = openai/gpt-5
input.agentConfig.subagents.model    = (unset)
agent-scope source                   = default-subagent
agent-scope raw                      = "openai/gpt-5"
model-selection.resolveConfigured()  = openai/gpt-5
model-selection.resolveSpawn()       = openai/gpt-5
OK scenario A — global default beats agent primary

--- scenario B — per-agent override wins ---
input.agentConfig.model              = anthropic/claude-opus
input.defaults.subagents.model       = openai/gpt-5
input.agentConfig.subagents.model    = openai/gpt-5-mini
agent-scope source                   = subagent
agent-scope raw                      = "openai/gpt-5-mini"
model-selection.resolveConfigured()  = openai/gpt-5-mini
model-selection.resolveSpawn()       = openai/gpt-5-mini
OK scenario B — per-agent override wins

--- scenario C — falls through to agent primary ---
input.agentConfig.model              = anthropic/claude-opus
input.defaults.subagents.model       = (unset)
input.agentConfig.subagents.model    = (unset)
agent-scope source                   = agent
agent-scope raw                      = "anthropic/claude-opus"
model-selection.resolveConfigured()  = anthropic/claude-opus
model-selection.resolveSpawn()       = anthropic/claude-opus
OK scenario C — falls through to agent primary

PROOF PASSED — subagent model precedence follows: per-agent > defaults.subagents > agent primary
```

For completeness, the Vitest suites listed in Test plan also pass on this branch (`pnpm test src/agents/model-selection.test.ts` → 105 passed; `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent.model-formatting.test.ts` → 32 passed; etc.), but the authoritative proof above is the live `node --import tsx` run against the actual patched source files, not the unit suite.

**Observed result after fix:**

- Scenario A (Opus main agent, `defaults.subagents.model = openai/gpt-5`, no per-agent override): the live resolver returns `openai/gpt-5` with `source = default-subagent`. Before this PR it returned `anthropic/claude-opus` with `source = agent` — the exact bug from #58822.
- Scenario B (per-agent `subagents.model = openai/gpt-5-mini` set alongside the global default): `openai/gpt-5-mini` with `source = subagent` wins. Per-agent override still beats the global default, confirming the highest-priority rung is intact.
- Scenario C (neither per-agent `subagents.model` nor `defaults.subagents.model` set): resolves to the agent's own `model` (`anthropic/claude-opus`) with `source = agent`, confirming the documented last-resort fallback is preserved.

The `node` exit code was `0` (proof script asserts each expectation; first mismatch would throw). The `agent-scope` source labels are produced by the shared `resolveSubagentModelConfigSelectionResult` helper used by both `src/agents/model-selection.ts` and `src/cron/isolated-agent/model-selection.ts`, so a passing scope source label means the cron path inherits the same precedence.

**What was not tested:** Cross-OS runtime (macOS / Windows) — code path is platform-agnostic config resolution, no filesystem or shell dependencies. Live spawn of an actual subagent process against a remote model provider — the resolver returns the same model ref regardless of whether the downstream spawn succeeds, and the spawn path is exercised by `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts` (11 passing). UI surfacing of the resolved subagent model in the gateway is also unchanged by this PR and not re-tested here.

- show gateway probe duration in the standard text output for `openclaw health`

## Changes
- print `Gateway probe duration: <n>ms` in text output
- add/update tests to lock the behavior

## Linked issue
- Closes #50245

## Test plan
- `pnpm test src/commands/health.test.ts src/commands/health.command.coverage.test.ts`
